title: External Grafana routing mismatch after production deployment

postmortem.summary: Simulated incident for the Proyecto Integrador: the Grafana route exposed by Kubernetes did not match the external service configuration and required a corrective deployment.
postmortem.impact: Dashboard access was degraded while the route/service mismatch was corrected.
postmortem.trigger: Kubernetes manifest changes adjusted the external Grafana service naming and ingress route configuration without an automated route-level validation gate.
postmortem.detection: Manual post-deploy verification of the public /grafana route. The deployment rollout was green, but the Grafana route was not covered by required smoke tests.
postmortem.root_cause: The Kubernetes external-grafana service integration drifted from the route expected by the public ingress.
postmortem.resolution: Mitigation work associated with deployment 06e79dd restored dashboard access; durable follow-up 6dae69c normalized the production host and external Grafana service naming contract.

timeline.event: Commit 3c40b28 added external-grafana-service and exposed /grafana through Ingress.
Commit 7c15ca5 changed the Grafana service, endpoint, and ingress backend name to grafana.
Public Grafana route failed post-deploy verification; incident opened as SEV2.
Team identified that rollout status did not validate the external dashboard route.
Public Grafana access restored; MTTR recorded for DORA.
Commit 6dae69c made the durable manifest follow-up by normalizing production host and service naming.

five_whys.why: Why was Grafana unavailable through the public URL?
five_whys.answer: The public /grafana route did not resolve to the expected external Grafana backend after ingress/service configuration changes.

five_whys.why: Why did the route point to an invalid or unexpected backend/host contract?
five_whys.answer: Multiple manifests controlled the Grafana route, service, endpoint, and redirect, and they were changed without one automated contract check covering all of them.

five_whys.why: Why did the deployment pipeline allow that change to reach production?
five_whys.answer: The pipeline waited for backend/frontend rollout status, but rollout status does not prove that an external Ingress path is reachable.

five_whys.why: Why was there no route-level validation?
five_whys.answer: The smoke-test design focused on the application path and treated observability routes as supporting infrastructure, not production-facing reliability dependencies.

five_whys.why: Why was the observability route not treated as a release contract?
five_whys.answer: Ownership and acceptance criteria for externally exposed monitoring endpoints were not documented in the deployment runbook or CI/CD quality gates.

action_items.action: Restore public Grafana route and normalize production host/service configuration.
action_items.success_criteria: The public /grafana route returns a valid Grafana response or redirect.

action_items.action: Make post-deploy smoke tests mandatory for /, /grafana/, and the SLO dashboard URL in production.
action_items.success_criteria: Production deploy fails automatically if any required route returns non-2xx/3xx or times out.

action_items.action: Add Kubernetes manifest validation for Ingress backend service names and ports after rendering manifests.
action_items.success_criteria: CI fails when an Ingress backend references a Service or port that is absent from the rendered manifests.

action_items.action: Add a blackbox HTTP probe and alert for the public Grafana route.
action_items.success_criteria: Alert fires when /grafana/ is unavailable for more than 2 minutes.

action_items.action: Document the external Grafana route contract in monitoring/README.md.
action_items.success_criteria: README lists the public URL, Kubernetes Service/Endpoints object names, expected host, and validation commands.

follow_up: Add an automated smoke test for /grafana/ after production deploys.
Keep Grafana service and ingress names covered by manifest validation.
Record production incidents in this file so CFR and MTTR stay auditable.
